title: Fix backend owner authorization — role validation rejecting valid Owner principal

requirements.text: Fix the backend owner role validation logic in backend/main.mo so that the authenticated Internet Identity principal stored as the owner at initialization is correctly compared against the caller principal in all protected functions (addWorker, addHoliday, markAttendance, and any other owner-gated calls). The comparison must use structural principal equality, not string coercion or any other indirect method that could silently fail.

quotes: backend rejects every protected action with: 'Unauthorized: Only the owner can add workers'
Fix role-based middleware so Owner role is recognized.
Remove any incorrect hardcoded role checks.

acceptanceCriteria: The owner principal captured at canister initialization (or first-time setup) is persisted in stable storage.
All protected functions compare msg.caller directly to the stored owner principal using Principal.equal or equivalent.
No hardcoded principal literals or string-based role checks remain in the authorization path.
Calling addWorker, addHoliday, and markAttendance as the Owner principal succeeds without an Unauthorized error.

requirements.text: Audit and fix the owner initialization flow in backend/main.mo to ensure the owner principal is correctly recorded when the canister is first set up. If the owner principal is stored via a setup/register call, verify that it is written to stable storage and survives canister upgrades. If it was never written (null/unset), add a safe re-initialization path that allows the current authenticated caller to claim ownership when no owner is set.

quotes: Ensure Owner account is correctly created in database.

acceptanceCriteria: Owner principal is stored in a stable variable so it persists across upgrades.
If the stored owner principal is unset (null), the first authenticated caller can claim the owner role.
Subsequent calls to claim ownership are rejected once an owner is already recorded.
The owner principal is never reset to null by a canister upgrade.

requirements.text: Verify that the frontend actor hook (useActor.ts / useQueries.ts) always constructs the backend actor with the authenticated Internet Identity agent, so that msg.caller on the backend receives the real owner principal and not the anonymous principal. If any mutation call (addWorker, addHoliday, attendance mark) is invoked before the actor is fully initialized with the identity, add a guard that waits for actor readiness before dispatching the call.

quotes: Ensure JWT / session token is passed in all API requests.

acceptanceCriteria: The actor used in all mutation hooks is initialized with the authenticated Internet Identity agent, not an anonymous agent.
No mutation is dispatched while the actor is in an uninitialized or anonymous state.
The principal logged client-side for the actor matches the owner principal stored in the backend.

constraints: Do not regenerate or redesign the application.
Do not change any UI layout, styling, or features.
Do not alter any immutable frontend paths (useActor.ts, useInternetIdentity.ts, main.tsx, ui components).
Scope all changes strictly to the authorization/role-validation logic.
nonGoals: UI redesign or visual changes of any kind.
Changing the data model beyond what is required to persist the owner principal correctly.
Modifying worker login or PIN authentication flows.
